statement.markdown: Given a set of strings, insert them into a trie and count the number of leaves in the trie after all insertions. However, be cautious, as the implementation of the Trie class contains a bug that must be fixed.
statement.inputFormat: A set of strings, one per line, where each string is at most 100 characters long.
statement.outputFormat: The total number of leaves in the trie.
statement.notes: The input size will not exceed 10000 strings, and the length of each string will not exceed 100 characters.

examples.explanation: The trie will have three leaves, for the words 'apple', 'banana', and 'cherry'.
The trie will have two leaves, for the words 'hello' and 'world'.

hints.text: The bug is related to the construction of the trie.

editorial.title: Trie Construction and Leaf Counting
editorial.intuition: Use a trie data structure to efficiently store and count the leaves.
editorial.bruteForce: Construct the trie by inserting each string into it and then count the leaves.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a recursive insertion function to insert each string into the trie and count the leaves.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution correctly inserts each string into the trie and counts the leaves.

starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int countLeafs() { /* TODO: implement */ } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def count_leafs(self) -> int: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class TrieNode { public: TrieNode* children[26]; TrieNode* parent; }; class Solution { public: int countLeafs() { int count = 0; for (const auto& node : nodes) { if (node->children[0] != nullptr) count++; } return count; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class TrieNode { public: TrieNode[] children; TrieNode parent; }; public class Solution { public int countLeafs() { int count = 0; for (TrieNode node : nodes) { if (node.children[0] != null) count++; } return count; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class TrieNode: pass; class Solution: def count_leafs(self) -> int: count = 0; for node in self.nodes: if node.children[0]: count++; return count;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: class TrieNode { constructor() { this.children = []; this.parent = null; } } function countLeafs() { let count = 0; for (const node of nodes) { if (node.children[0]) count++; } return count; }
